commonIssues:
Mandatory biometric verification at fair price shops has led to many beneficiaries losing access to rations if their Aadhaar is not linked or if e-KYC fails.

How to fix:

  • Visit the nearest CSC (Common Service Center) or Jan Seva Kendra to link your Aadhaar with your ration card and complete the biometrics verification.
  • If biometrics fail due to age or work, request the dealer to use the mobile OTP authentication method provided by the UP Food and Logistics Department.

The state government has conducted drives to identify and remove "ineligible" cardholders, leading to the cancellation of lakhs of cards. Residents complain about wrongful deletion of eligible families.

How to fix:

  • Check the current status of your card on the Food and Logistics Department's portal (fcs.up.gov.in).
  • If deleted wrongly, submit a written appeal along with fresh income and residence proof to the local Supply Inspector or Tehsil office for reconsideration.

Residents face difficulties in updating their family details, particularly in adding new members (newborns or brides) or removing married daughters.

How to fix:

  • Apply for name addition/removal through the e-District UP portal or visit a Jan Seva Kendra with relevant certificates (Birth/Marriage Certificate/Aadhaar).
  • Follow up with the local Supply Office if the correction is not reflected in the online list within 30 working days.

Fair price shop dealers are frequently reported to be charging more than the official rate, especially under the Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY). Some dealers also provide less than the allotted 5 kg per person.

How to fix:

  • Always check the digital weighing machine display and ensure the dealer matches the weight exactly to the printed receipt on the ePOS machine.
  • Lodge a direct complaint on the UP government's toll-free helpline number 1800-1800-150 or use the 'Jansunwai-Samadhan' portal.

Technical glitches in the electronic point of sale (ePOS) machines and server issues often result in "transaction failed" messages, denying ration to migrants.

How to fix:

  • Try the transaction later or ask the dealer to restart the machine to clear server cache issues.
  • If rejection persists, migrants can use the 'Mera Ration' app to locate the nearest stable FPS shop or register a transfer of their ration quota.

Many families risk losing their ration cards because the income criteria for eligibility has not been revised in over a decade, despite inflation.

How to fix:

  • Ensure your latest Income Certificate is correctly updated on the e-District portal and submitted to the Supply Office.
  • Prepare a fresh declaration of all family assets and present it to the Village Accountant or Lekhpal if a re-survey is ordered.

In some rural areas, residents face long delays in getting new cards (ranging from 4–18 months) and complain about exploitation by middlemen.

How to fix:

  • Avoid unofficial middlemen. All Ration Card services are now available digitaly through fcs.up.gov.in and officially sanctioned Jan Seva Kendras.
  • If an application is stalled for many months, lodge a grievance on the CM Helpline (1076) to trigger an official probe into the delay.
